concept_query: How does the pH scale work?

timeline.title: Introduction to pH
timeline.voiceover_script: Welcome! pH stands for 'potential of Hydrogen'. It is a scale used to specify the acidity or basicity of an aqueous solution.

timeline.title: The Scale Breakdown
timeline.voiceover_script: The scale ranges from 0 to 14. A pH of 7 is neutral, like pure water. Values below 7 are acidic - they have more hydrogen ions. Values above 7 are basic, or alkaline.
visual_elements: Highlighting pH 1 Acid (Red) and pH 14 Base (Purple)

timeline.title: Logarithmic Nature
timeline.voiceover_script: The pH scale is logarithmic. Each whole pH value below 7 is ten times more acidic than the next higher value. For example, pH 5 is ten times more acidic than pH 6.
visual_elements: Magnifying glass zooming into molecular concentration difference

meta_tags.note: Full LLM+TTS integration recommended for production. Use GPT-4 Vision for semantic validation and Runway/Sora for animated visuals.
